expectations.text: Applies the three business thresholds correctly (any ONE suffices): annual gross revenue above the $25M threshold (as CPI-adjusted); buying/selling/sharing PI of 100,000+ consumers or households (CPRA raised this from 50,000); or 50%+ of revenue from selling/sharing PI — and concludes the company IS subject via the revenue threshold ($30M > $25M) even though 80,000 < 100,000
expectations.evidence: Table lists all three thresholds ($25M revenue, 100,000+ consumers/households, 50%+ revenue from PI sale/sharing) and states 'Because you clear the $25M revenue threshold on its own, you are a covered business regardless of the fact that your California customer volume (80,000/year) falls short of the 100,000-consumer/household threshold. Only one threshold needs to be met, not all three.'

expectations.text: Notes the 'doing business in California' element (for-profit, collects CA residents' PI, determines purposes/means) — being US-based outside California does not avoid the law
expectations.evidence: Line 5: 'CCPA/CPRA applies to a for-profit business doing business in California if it meets at least one of three thresholds' — states the for-profit/doing-business-in-CA gate, though it does not explicitly spell out that being US-based outside California doesn't avoid the law.

expectations.text: Lists core obligations: notice at collection, privacy policy, consumer request handling (know/delete/correct/opt-out/limit), verifiable request processes, service-provider contracts, non-discrimination
expectations.evidence: Sections '2. Privacy Notices' (Notice at Collection, Privacy Policy), rights table (Know/Delete/Correct/Opt-Out/Limit SPI/Non-Discrimination), 'Identity verification (tiered)' section, and '4. Vendor / Downstream Classification' covering service-provider contracts all present.

expectations.text: Mentions the 45-day response deadline for consumer requests with a possible 45-day extension
expectations.evidence: Table row: 'Response timeline (applies to all request types): confirm receipt within 10 business days... then substantively respond within 45 calendar days (extendable once by another 45 days with notice to the consumer).'

expectations.text: Mentions opt-out of sale/sharing mechanics: 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link and honoring opt-out preference signals such as GPC
expectations.evidence: Section '3. Opt-Out Mechanisms': '"Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" link on your homepage. Honor Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as a valid opt-out — this is CPPA-confirmed and mandatory, not optional.'
